title: Clarify Requirements
description: Interactive requirements gathering for a universal portfolio + blog template that adapts to any persona — from ML engineers to photographers to consultants — deployed on Vercel using TypeScript, HTML, CSS, and optional Next.js.
instructions: Begin a structured interview with the user. Ask one section at a time. Do not dump all questions at once.

Section A — Persona Scope: Who are the intended end-users of the template? Is it truly persona-agnostic or does it have a primary target (technical vs creative vs professional)? What is the minimum viable persona set to design for?

Section B — Portfolio Domain: What types of work items need to be showcased? (Projects, case studies, artworks, publications, code repos, videos, PDFs?) Should work items support rich media or text-only? Is there a need for password-protected or private work items?

Section C — Blog Domain: What content types does the blog support? (Long-form articles, short notes, tutorials, image galleries?) Is MDX (Markdown + JSX) the authoring format or is a CMS (Sanity, Contentful) preferred? Are code syntax highlighting and math rendering required?

Section D — Identity & Customisation: How deeply should a new user be able to customise the template? (Colours, fonts, layout, sections?) Is there a config-file-driven customisation system or a UI-based one? Should themes be switchable at runtime or build time?

Section E — Navigation & Structure: What are the required top-level pages? (Home, About, Work/Portfolio, Blog, Contact?) Are single-page-app scroll sections needed or multi-page routing? Is a CV/Resume download feature required?

Section F — Integrations: Is contact form functionality needed (email, Resend, Formspree)? Are analytics required (Vercel Analytics, Plausible, PostHog)? Is RSS feed generation needed for the blog? Is sitemap.xml auto-generation needed for SEO?

Section G — Deployment Constraints: The target is Vercel Edge. Are there ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) requirements? Is a monorepo structure (Turborepo) needed? Are environment variables needed for any integrations?
After all sections: Summarise findings as a structured requirements document. Highlight any conflicts or ambiguities. Ask the user to confirm before proceeding to domain decomposition.
outputs: A list of confirmed in-scope features
A list of explicitly out-of-scope features
Open questions log for deferred decisions
guardrails: Do not assume the user is technical. Use plain language in all questions.
Do not begin architecture or code decisions during this phase.
Do not conflate portfolio and blog requirements — treat them as separate domains that share infrastructure.
